item_a.name: Casper Original Mattress
item_a.image_alt: Casper Original all-foam mattress with zoned support layers on white background
pros: Excellent pressure relief with three-layer foam construction
cons: Can sleep warmer than hybrid alternatives
Edge support is weaker than innerspring models
Off-gassing odor during first few days
item_a.best_for: Side sleepers and couples who prioritize motion isolation and pressure relief in a convenient all-foam package.

item_b.name: Saatva Classic Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Saatva Classic luxury innerspring mattress with Euro pillow top and dual coil system
pros: Dual-coil construction provides excellent support and durability
Three firmness options (Plush Soft, Luxury Firm, Firm)
Superior edge support and bounce from innerspring design
Free white glove delivery and old mattress removal included
cons: Higher price point than bed-in-a-box competitors
More motion transfer than all-foam mattresses
Heavier weight (80-140 lbs) makes repositioning difficult
item_b.best_for: Back and stomach sleepers seeking a traditional innerspring feel with luxury hotel-style comfort and premium service.

comparison_summary: Casper and Saatva represent opposite design philosophies. Casper built its reputation on affordable foam comfort with great motion isolation, while Saatva delivers luxury innerspring construction with superior breathability and edge support at nearly double the price. Your sleep position and budget make this choice pretty straightforward.
verdict_text: Saatva wins for most people thanks to better durability, support, and temperature control, but side sleepers on a tight budget should grab the Casper.

key_differences.item_b: Dual-coil hybrid (recycled steel coils + memory foam lumbar pad)
Three options: Plush Soft (4/10), Luxury Firm (6/10), Firm (8/10)
Moderate - some motion transfer due to coils
Excellent - foam-encased perimeter with 8-9 inch usable edge

key_differences.item_a: Moderate - perforated foam but retains some heat
key_differences.item_b: Excellent - breathable coil system with organic cotton cover
White glove delivery with setup and old mattress removal

seo_content:
I've tested dozens of mattresses over the years, and the Casper vs Saatva matchup represents one of the clearest divisions in the industry. These companies took completely opposite paths to success. Casper launched the bed-in-a-box revolution with its all-foam Original, while Saatva decided traditional innerspring mattresses just needed better pricing and delivery.

The Casper Original uses three layers of foam with zoned support that cradles pressure points while keeping your spine aligned. The memory foam comfort layer does an excellent job with motion isolation—if your partner moves around at night, you won't feel it. The medium-firm feel (around 6 out of 10) works well for most side sleepers and combination sleepers, though stomach sleepers typically need more support. Pricing runs $695-$1,395 depending on size, which is competitive for the foam mattress category.

Saatva takes a different approach entirely. The Saatva Classic features a dual-coil system with individually wrapped comfort coils on top of a tempered steel base layer. This construction delivers exceptional edge support, superior breathability, and that responsive bounce many people prefer over foam. They added a lumbar zone with memory foam for targeted lower back support. What I really appreciate is that Saatva offers three firmness levels—Plush Soft, Luxury Firm, and Firm—giving you more options than Casper's single feel. The tradeoff? Prices range from $1,395-$2,695, nearly double what you'll pay for Casper.

Temperature regulation isn't even close. Saatva's coil-on-coil design allows air to flow freely, while Casper's foam layers trap heat despite perforations. Hot sleepers consistently rate Saatva cooler. Motion isolation flips the script—Casper's foam absorbs movement better than Saatva's interconnected coils.

Delivery experiences are totally different. Casper arrives compressed in a box for DIY setup, perfect for apartment dwellers or anyone who wants their mattress immediately. Saatva includes complimentary white glove delivery with professional setup and old mattress removal, though you'll need to schedule it.

Durability favors Saatva. Steel coil construction typically outlasts all-foam mattresses. Owners report 10+ years of support from Saatva, while foam mattresses often develop body impressions after 7-8 years. Both offer industry-standard warranties, but Saatva's construction suggests better long-term value despite the higher upfront cost.

Your decision should factor in sleep position, budget, and what matters most to you. Side sleepers on a budget will love Casper's pressure relief and value pricing. Back sleepers, stomach sleepers, heavier individuals, and anyone who sleeps hot should invest in Saatva's superior support and breathability.

faq.q: Is Saatva worth the extra cost compared to Casper?
faq.a: For most people, yes. The dual-coil construction lasts longer, sleeps cooler, and gives you better edge support. Plus you get white glove delivery included, which adds $200+ in value. But if you're a side sleeper under 200 lbs on a tight budget, Casper performs well enough at half the price.

faq.q: Which mattress is better for side sleepers, Casper or Saatva?
faq.a: Casper usually works better for side sleepers under 200 lbs because the foam layers cushion your shoulders and hips nicely. Saatva's Plush Soft option also works for side sleepers, especially if you're over 200 lbs and need more support. Just avoid the Luxury Firm and Firm models if you sleep on your side—they'll create pressure points.

faq.q: Do Casper and Saatva mattresses sleep hot?
faq.a: Saatva sleeps much cooler because the coil-on-coil design lets air flow constantly. Casper traps more heat like all-foam mattresses do, though the perforated foam helps a bit. If you sleep hot, go with Saatva or look at Casper's hybrid models instead of the all-foam Original.

faq.q: How long do Casper and Saatva mattresses last?
faq.a: Saatva typically lasts 10-12 years thanks to the durable steel coils. Casper's all-foam design averages 7-9 years before you'll see body impressions forming. Both have comparable warranties, but the materials in Saatva just resist sagging longer than foam does.

faq.q: Which has better edge support, Casper or Saatva?
faq.a: Saatva wins by a lot. The foam-encased perimeter and innerspring construction give you 8-9 inches of usable surface area. Casper's foam edges compress more easily, reducing usable space to about 5-6 inches from the edge. This matters if you're sharing the bed or like to sit on the edge while getting dressed.

faq.q: Is Casper Original Mattress better than Saatva Classic Mattress?
faq.a: Not for most people—Saatva wins overall thanks to superior durability, support, and temperature control. However, Casper is the better choice for side sleepers on a budget who prioritize affordability and motion isolation over luxury features.

faq.q: Should I buy Casper Original Mattress or Saatva Classic Mattress?
faq.a: Choose Saatva if you want the best all-around mattress with excellent breathability, edge support, and long-term durability. Go with Casper if you're a side sleeper on a tight budget and value affordable foam comfort with great motion isolation.

faq.q: What is the difference between Casper Original Mattress and Saatva Classic Mattress?
faq.a: Casper uses affordable foam construction optimized for motion isolation and comfort, while Saatva employs luxury innerspring technology for superior breathability and edge support. Saatva costs nearly double the price but delivers better durability and temperature control, making it the premium choice for most sleepers.

meta_title: Casper vs Saatva (2026): Foam vs Innerspring Compared
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Casper vs Saatva mattresses. We break down construction, support, temperature regulation, and value to help you pick the right...
comparison_intro: If you're torn between the Casper Original Mattress vs Saatva Classic Mattress, you're not alone—both are solid choices, but understanding which is better for your needs requires looking at the key differences. The main difference between Casper Original Mattress and Saatva Classic Mattress comes down to durability, support layers, and how well each sleeps cool, which is where our detailed comparison of Casper Original Mattress compared to Saatva Classic Mattress becomes invaluable. Whether you're deciding on a Casper Original Mattress or Saatva Classic Mattress, this guide breaks down the pros and cons so you can make a confident choice based on your sleep style and budget.
